subject: Adding AAF configuration
commit_message:
Adding AAF configuration

Adding the current AAF configuration files and certificates

Change-Id: I2288c9af7be021a993e99441961831b6a0d763e6
Issue-ID: AAI-32
Signed-off-by: Pavel Paroulek <pavel.paroulek@orange.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-master-verify-helm/412/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/oom-master-verify-helm/412

Patch Set 1:

Hi, for some reason I don't have the original change-id.

This change updates the keyfile to k8s secrets as pointed out by Harish

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

A few questions..
- I see many "AAF_LOCATE_URL" strings in URLs..should this be a real IP or dns name?
- It seems like some of the certs and stuff are copies from the AAF chart.  Did you investigate ways of sharing what is already in AAF rather than having a fork under aai-resources?
- does this introduce a dependency on a particular AAF microservice within aai-resources?  if it does then for it to function properly, you may consider adding a ready.py init container check to prevent the start up of aai-resources until the appropriate aaf component is running.

Patch Set 1:

minor: 
-There are some red trailing whitespaces..if you feel like removing them please do..

-there are many commented out properties that are duplicates...these really have no value in a k8s config map so go ahead and remove them or replace them with comments documenting the alternative values for them.

Patch Set 2: Verified+1

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-master-verify-helm/422/ : SUCCESS

Logs: https://logs.onap.org/production/vex-yul-ecomp-jenkins-1/oom-master-verify-helm/422

Patch Set 2:

> A few questions..
 > - I see many "AAF_LOCATE_URL" strings in URLs..should this be a
 > real IP or dns name?
 > - It seems like some of the certs and stuff are copies from the AAF
 > chart.  Did you investigate ways of sharing what is already in AAF
 > rather than having a fork under aai-resources?
 > - does this introduce a dependency on a particular AAF microservice
 > within aai-resources?  if it does then for it to function properly,
 > you may consider adding a ready.py init container check to prevent
 > the start up of aai-resources until the appropriate aaf component
 > is running.

- AAF_LOCATE_URL is an AAF internal placeholder, not changed under normal circumstances
- certs and files were generated for us by the AAF team from their tool and were given "as is". The only suspicious file is truststoreONAPall.jks but I suspect it contains AAI specific cert (but I might be wrong there)
- that is a good idea and we will do it in the next interation. AAF is not enabled for now, these are just properties needed by out WS

Patch Set 2:

> minor:
 > -There are some red trailing whitespaces..if you feel like removing
 > them please do..
 > 
 > -there are many commented out properties that are duplicates...these
 > really have no value in a k8s config map so go ahead and remove
 > them or replace them with comments documenting the alternative
 > values for them.

- The trailing whitespaces have been removed
- commented out props have been removed, although change of config will probably happen to a whole file, not a single property as these config files are generated and replaced as a whole (but that might change in the future..)
Change has been successfully merged by Mandeep Khinda

Patch Set 2:

Build Successful 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/oom-master-merge-helm/90/ : SUCCESS
